claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility properly, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and lives with you at home.

Since Alex is 17, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he at school, college, or doing an apprenticeship or training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 17 and in approved education, he would generally qualify under the age criteria.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or living away from home for any reason?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He lives here at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old
- Alex is studying for his A-levels (approved education)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you at home full-time
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care

According to the official UK government guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 17, lives with you, and is in approved education (studying for A-levels), you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is under 20 years old (he's 17)
- He's in approved education (studying for A-levels)
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If his circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education), you should notify HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and studying for his A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, a claimant normally qualifies for Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age criteria as he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives in the UK with Alex, is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child. There are no circumstances that would disqualify the claim.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and studying for his A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, a claimant normally qualifies for Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age criteria as he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives in the UK with Alex, is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child. There are no circumstances that would disqualify the claim.
